assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md contains '## Classification: Code Change Request' and '## Action: Create sub-task (subtask-30001.md) to implement the transaction wrapping.' The file subtask-30001.md exists in the outputs directory. The report table row for comment 30001 shows 'Code change request' classification and 'Yes -- subtask-30001.md' for sub-task.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains '## Classification: Suggestion' and the Reasoning section explicitly calls out the suggestive language: '"should also add" -- the phrase "should also" indicates an additive suggestion rather than a mandatory fix' and '"would help" -- this is conditional/advisory language, not a directive.' The reasoning explains why each phrase is suggestive rather than directive.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md contains '## Classification: Nit' and '## Action: No sub-task created. This is a minor style nit for the author to optionally address.' No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory. The report table shows 'Nit' classification and 'No' for sub-task.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md contains '## Classification: Question' and '## Action: No sub-task created. This is a question for the PR author to answer in the review thread.' No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory. The report table shows 'Question' classification and 'No' for sub-task.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (trustify-backend), '## Description' (wrapping UPDATE statements in transaction), '## Files to Modify' (modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs), '## Implementation Notes' (4 bullet points on transaction implementation), and '## Acceptance Criteria' (4 checkbox items). Only one sub-task was created (subtask-30001.md), so 'each' applies only to this file.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' section with the exact URL 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' on line 29.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' section (lines 31-34) which includes 'reviewer-a on modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs line 60:' followed by the full quoted original review comment text: 'The soft_delete method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only: report.md, review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md, and subtask-30001.md. All are markdown documentation/report files. No source code files (.rs, .sql, .toml, etc.) were created or appear in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have individual classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md) and all four appear in the report's Review Comment Summary table with classifications and sub-task decisions. The report covers all comments comprehensively.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md contains no mention of merging, auto-merging, or any merge action. It ends with '*This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr*.' The report presents verification results and sub-tasks but does not take any merge action.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: Report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | `tests/api/sbom_delete.rs` is a new file |'. Also in the detailed section (lines 107-108): '#### Test Change Classification: ADDITIVE' and '`tests/api/sbom_delete.rs` is a new file (created from `/dev/null`). No existing tests were modified or removed.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a '## Convention Upgrade Evaluation' section explaining: 'The suggestion was evaluated for convention upgrade eligibility. For a suggestion to be upgraded to a code change request, it must match a documented project convention... the fixture data does not include the contents of CONVENTIONS.md and there is no documented convention requiring indexes on soft-delete columns or any specific indexing strategy. Without a matching documented convention, this suggestion cannot be upgraded.' The report also contains a '#### Convention Upgrade Evaluation (Comment 30002)' section with similar analysis.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md Action section states: 'No sub-task created. This remains a suggestion for the author to consider.' No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory. The report table shows Comment 30002 classified as 'Suggestion' with 'No' for sub-task. The convention upgrade evaluation in review-30002.md confirms: 'Without a matching documented convention, this suggestion cannot be upgraded.'

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: Report.md line 13 (Test Quality row): 'Eval Quality: N/A.' Lines 102-104 in the detailed section: '#### Eval Quality: N/A' followed by 'The 3-criteria detection was applied (author `github-actions[bot]`, marker `## Eval Results`, footer `sdlc-workflow/run-evals`). No matches found -- this is not an eval PR.' All three detection criteria are explicitly named and the result is N/A.
